technique.name: Integration Check
technique.description: Did this actually metabolize? Check before and after. The only proof is changed behavior.

steps.text:
You sit with the Integration Check. This is not a feelings check. This is a proof check.

You came to this session with something. A decision made, a charge released, an insight earned. Now you're checking whether it moved through you or just passed through.

Before this work, what was the pattern?

Name it concretely. Not the feeling — the behavior. What did you do, say, or avoid? When did it show up? Who did it affect?

The body keeps the score. The behavior is the scoreboard.

Now. What is the current behavior?

Be specific. Did you do the thing you said you'd do? Did you say the thing you were afraid to say? Did you notice the charge and stay with it instead of discharging it onto someone else?

This is the only measure that matters.

The verdict is not good or bad. It is true or false.

If the behavior changed: the work metabolized. Something moved through. You earned it.

If the behavior didn't change: the work didn't complete. The insight stayed cognitive. The body held its pattern.

Both are information. Neither is a failure.

choices.text: If nothing changed — this is data, not failure

steps.text:
Something changed. The work metabolized.

You name what shifted: which behavior, how much, in what direction. You log it not as a trophy but as a record. The record is what lets you see the pattern over time.

You earned this. The changed behavior is the proof.

Nothing changed. The pattern held.

This is not a failure of the work — it is information about where the resistance is. The body chose its familiar pattern over the new learning. That choice is data.

You ask: where is the pattern still protective? What would happen if it let go?

choices.text: Name where the pattern is still protective

steps.text:
The Integration Check closes. You know what the work did and didn't do.

This is the practice. Not the big transformation — the small, repeated proof that change is actually happening.

The record is in you. The system remembers too.

You leave the Integration Check. The next time you do this work, the before state will be more recent. The gap between sessions is where the integration happens — or doesn't.

The check doesn't lie. Neither do you, when you do it.
